entries.what_they_read: Headline hit first: 'Trade player tokens that move with real stats.' Then the three-step flow and the $100 no-signup pitch. Scanned the LeBron $5.59 vs $4.20 example fast.
entries.their_interpretation: It's a paper-trading sim for NBA player pricing — stat-derived fair value vs. market price, you're arbing the gap. No real money, no wallet, no seed phrase — devnet wrapper, basically.
entries.their_rationale: No account friction, no seed phrase, instant $100 — zero cost to test the mechanic. The fair-value vs. market-price spread is a real tradeable concept and I want to see how tight the bid-ask actually is on a role player.
confusion_notes: 'The market decides what it actually trades at' — who is the market here? Other demo users? AMM? Unclear liquidity source.
'Buying raises the price for the next person' — sounds bonding-curve-ish but never named. Want to know the exact pricing function before I size up.
'AWAITING TIP-OFF' status bar — is this a live-data dependency? Will tokens be frozen until tip-off? Could affect entry timing.
No mention of position limits or max hold — matters for sizing strategy.
first_impression_elements: Big 'Claim $100 →' button — instant CTA, no friction listed
LeBron $5.59 vs $4.20 undervalued example — concrete, scannable, made the mechanic click immediately
'No account yet · $100 · no real money · no seed phrase' badge — killed the trust concern in one line
ignored_elements: '↓Terms explained' link — skipped, it's a devnet demo, not deploying capital
About · Methodology · Invite footer links — back-burner, will check Methodology after I see the live card data
'Practice mode. No real money, no financial risk, no financial advice.' disclaimer — registered it peripherally, didn't read

first_impression_elements: Orange 'real stats.' headline — high contrast, eye rip straight to value prop
Yellow CTA button — stands out hard against dark grid bg, immediate action signal
DEVNET / PRACTICE MODE nav tags — flags this is pre-prod, raises eyebrows
ignored_elements: Grid background texture — decorative noise, zero signal
'AWAITING TIP-OFF' ticker bar — saw it, dismissed it
data.visual_notes: Dark theme with tight typographic hierarchy reads competent — someone knows what they're doing with contrast and scale. But the DEVNET label and 'no real money' disclaimer stack up fast as trust killers for anyone looking to actually trade; feels beta-at-best. Zero social proof, no logo mark beyond wordmark, no product screenshot — thin inventory for a financial-adjacent pitch.
